currentTask: Fix the /api/customers Supabase permission error by enforcing a real service-role admin client in backend routes and repairing the createCustomer flow without touching the frontend UI.
notes: User requested backend-only Supabase repair work with UI left unchanged.
Patched env validation to support SUPABASE_URL fallback, reject placeholder values, and reject accidental NEXT_PUBLIC service-role exposure.
API failures now return actionable 503 messages for Supabase config/connection issues instead of collapsing everything to a generic 500.
Added repository fixes for database-side revenue filtering and idempotent invoice creation/backlink repair on unique-key races.
Added scripts/check-supabase.mjs and npm script supabase:check to validate envs, test connectivity, insert a temporary customer, and clean it up.
Verification result: npm run lint and npx tsc --noEmit pass; live Supabase check is blocked because no local Supabase env vars are set in this workspace.
Current pass is focused on the concrete runtime error 'permission denied for schema public' during POST /api/customers.

Code inspection shows repository writes already use getSupabaseAdminClient(), so the main suspected break is invalid service-role configuration or insufficient admin-client hardening rather than a frontend call path.

Patched src/lib/supabase.ts to reject JWT keys whose role claim is not service_role and to build the server client with explicit public schema plus Authorization/apikey headers from SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY.
Patched /api/customers POST to run validateConnection before insert and upgraded customer-facing Supabase permission-denied errors to a clearer admin-access/config failure.
Verification for the permission-error fix: npm run lint and npx tsc --noEmit pass on 2026-03-22.
